rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with proper structure and scoping.
generic_rules: Assumptions should reflect known constraints on input data or system behavior.
Guarantees should express expected outputs or system properties based on inputs.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with appropriate types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid identifiers in the component scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should match the types of the referenced variables.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
Implementation-level subcomponent paths in type contracts
